  html:
    theme: cosmo
    css: styles.css
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    embed-resources: true
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        .quarto-secondary-nav .quarto-container {
          display: flex;
          justify-content: space-between;
          align-items: center;
        }
        #github-link-nav {
          font-size: 0.8rem;
          text-decoration: none;
          color: #666;
          border: 1px solid #ddd;
          padding: 2px 8px;
          margin-top: 10px;
          display: inline-block;
          transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }
        #github-link-nav:hover {
          background-color: #f8f9fa;
          color: #000;
          border-color: #bbb;
        }
        </style>
    include-after-body:
      text: |
        <script>
        document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", function() {
          var toc = document.getElementById("TOC");
          if (toc) {
            var githubDiv = document.createElement("div");
            githubDiv.style.marginTop = "1.5rem";
            githubDiv.style.paddingTop = "1rem";
            githubDiv.style.borderTop = "1px solid #eee";
            githubDiv.innerHTML = '<a href="https://github.com/yichao2022/Behavioral-Digital-Twin-Roadmap" target="_blank" style="font-size: 0.85rem; color: #666; text-decoration: none;"><i class="bi bi-github"></i> Source Code</a>';
            toc.appendChild(githubDiv);
          }
        });
        </script>
---

## 1. Vision: Disaggregated Population Modeling

As a **PhD Researcher** specialized in structural behavioral models and administrative burden, I propose a high-granularity approach to tobacco regulatory science. By modeling the individual-level utility surface, we can simulate complex transitions between products (Cigarettes, E-cigs, HTPs) and predict the equity impacts of policy interventions before they manifest.

Traditional aggregate models often miss the non-linear "churn" in poly-use. My research focus is on building **Behavioral Simulation Models** (or "Digital Twins") that bridge longitudinal survey data with real-time behavioral signals to provide a robust, independent audit for regulatory decisions.

## 2. Core Research Synergies

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
### BDT Logic Flow: From Data to Policy Audit
```{mermaid}
graph TD
    A[Raw Data: PATH, NHIS, XHS] --> B{BDT Engine}
    B --> C[Individual Level Utility Surface]
    B --> D[Disaggregated Agent Simulation]
    C & D --> E[Population Impact Projections]
    E --> F[Regulatory Decision Support]
    
    subgraph "Independent Audit Layer"
    G[Industry Model Assumptions] -.-> H[Logic & Causal Audit]
    H --> B
    end
    
    style G fill:#f96,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px
    style B fill:#bbf,stroke:#333,stroke-width:2px
```
:::

### A. Modeling Transitions & Poly-use Dynamics
Transitions are rarely binary. My BDT framework treats a user's tobacco journey as a **stochastic state-space model**.
- **Product Attributes**: I model how flavor bans or nicotine caps specifically alter the "utility surface" for users, driving them toward either cessation or alternative product uptake (e.g., switching from E-cigs back to combustibles).
- **Dynamic Poly-use**: Simulation of concurrent use patterns (Cigarette + Vaping) based on real-world longitudinal survey data.

### B. Precision Equity: Vulnerable Group Simulations
Aggregated data masks inequality. I specialize in **disaggregated agent modeling** for:
- **Low SES & Minority Groups**: Integrating specific behavioral constraints (financial burden, retail density) to predict how tax increases or marketing restrictions affect vulnerable groups differently than the general population.
- **Youth & Social Media Influence**: Utilizing ML to extract behavioral priors from social media (e.g., TikTok/XHS) to model the "initiation" phase for adolescents.

### C. Counter-Industry Predictive Modeling: The Independent Audit Tool

In response to industry-funded health impact models (e.g., PMI's population models), I advocate for **Independent, Transparent, and Robust Population Health Simulations**.

| Feature | Industry "Black-Box" Models | Structural Simulation (Our Model) |
|:---|:---:|:---:|
| **Transparency** | Proprietary / Hidden Causal Chains | **Open-Source / Peer-Audited** |
| **Granularity** | Aggregate cohorts | **Individual-level Agents** |
| **Bias Mitigation** | Optimized for commercial validation | **Optimized for Health Equity** |
| **Data Fusion** | Limited to historical trends | **Real-time Social Media + Survey Fusion** |

- **Logic Audit**: Applying structural logic to audit the causal assumptions in industry models.
- **Future Forecasting**: Providing evidence-based projections for FDA/State-level regulatory impact assessments.

## 3. Technical Stack for UCSF

- **Generative Agents**: Large-scale simulation of 100k+ independent tobacco-using agents.
- **ML & Social Media**: NLP pipelines for analyzing tobacco/cannabis sentiment and transition signals from unstandardized data.
- **Large Data Economics**: Expertise in longitudinal survey analysis (PATH, NHIS) integrated with structural modeling.

## 4. Proposed Roadmap at IHPS

1. **Phase 1**: Pilot a **Behavioral Simulation Model** using California-specific tobacco purchase data to simulate the impact of recent flavor restrictions on Low SES households.
2. **Phase 2**: Integrate social media sentiment analysis into transition probability matrices for emerging products (HTPs/Cannabis).
3. **Goal**: Establish a "Regulatory Sandbox" for policy evaluation that prioritizes health equity and independent oversight.

---

[Back to Portfolio](https://yichao2022.github.io)
